notes: Payment card industry security standards. PCI DSS is required for any organization that processes, stores, or transmits cardholder data.

match_nodes.description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

data.official_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
data.description: Security standard for organizations handling payment card data.
data.notes: Version 4.0.1 current (released June 2024, minor revision of 4.0 released March 2022). 12 requirements organized into 6 goals. Required for PCI compliance certification. Compliance is validated by Qualified Security Assessors (QSAs) or via Self-Assessment Questionnaires (SAQs). Requirement numbering uses dotted notation: top-level (1-12), sub-requirement (1.1), and specific controls (1.1.1). The standard document is freely downloadable from the PCI SSC document library.

versions_available.note: Minor revision of v4.0. Clarifications and corrections. 12 requirements, 6 goals.
Major revision from v3.2.1. Added customized approach option alongside defined approach.

urls.note: PCI SSC document library — standard is freely downloadable

children.description: PCI DSS top-level requirement (1-12). Each is a major security goal area.

data.note: 12 top-level requirements grouped into 6 goals: Build and Maintain a Secure Network (1-2), Protect Account Data (3-4), Maintain a Vulnerability Management Program (5-6), Implement Strong Access Control Measures (7-9), Regularly Monitor and Test Networks (10-11), Maintain an Information Security Policy (12).

examples.note: Install and maintain network security controls
Identify users and authenticate access to system components

known_values.1: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
known_values.2: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
known_values.4: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission Over Open, Public Networks
known_values.5: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
known_values.6: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
known_values.7: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
known_values.8: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
known_values.9: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
known_values.10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
known_values.11: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
known_values.12: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

children.description: PCI DSS sub-requirement (e.g., 1.1, 3.4). Groups related specific controls.

data.note: Sub-requirements group related specific controls under a top-level requirement.

examples.note: Processes and mechanisms for installing and maintaining network security controls
Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy cardholder data are restricted

children.description: PCI DSS specific control (e.g., 1.1.1, 8.3.1). The actionable control requirement.

data.note: Specific control requirements. Each has defined and customized approach options in v4.0+. No per-control web URL — refer to the downloadable standard document.

examples.note: All security policies and operational procedures identified in Requirement 1 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties
Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy cardholder data are restricted
All user access to system components for users and administrators is authenticated
